claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary audit via file snapshot comparison before/after agent execution, with enforcement blocking PRD readiness when mutations occur outside allowed artifact roots.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot computes SHA-256 digests for all project files under project root, excluding allowed write roots (output artifacts), creating a pre-execution baseline.
evidence.why: This function enables detection of any file mutations by comparison with post-execution state.

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after snapshots, returning list of added/modified/deleted files with change type.
evidence.why: This is the core boundary audit mechanism that detects unauthorized mutations.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called BEFORE agent execution loop to capture baseline state.
evidence.why: Establishes project state before any agents can potentially mutate files.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot called AFTER all agents complete, and diffBoundarySnapshots computes changes stored in result.boundary.
evidence.why: Post-execution audit detects any mutations that occurred during agent runs.

evidence.summary: Any boundary changes become blockers with code BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION, blocking PRD readiness.
evidence.why: Enforces boundary by converting detected mutations into PRD readiness blockers.

evidence.summary: execution_policy sets project_mutation forbidden and declares allowed_write_roots (typically .yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-*).
evidence.why: Policy declares which write roots are exempt from boundary audit checks.

evidence.summary: Agent hard rules explicitly forbid modifying target project files; must report verification gaps in missing array.
evidence.why: Documents the non-editing constraint enforced on all evidence agents.

evidence.summary: Every evidence record must declare scope (project/external/user/unknown).
evidence.why: Scope declaration enables project-fact validation requiring project-scoped evidence.

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues verifies project-scoped evidence includes repo-relative paths; returns blocker if missing.
evidence.why: Enforces that existing-project facts must cite concrete project locators.

evidence.summary: Evidence agent results pass through evidenceResultBlockingIssues and projectEvidenceBlockingIssues validation; blocking issues become readiness blockers.
evidence.why: Agent results are validated for both completion status and project evidence requirements.

assumptions: File digest computation works correctly for project files under git and non-git projects.
Agent providers (Claude -p, Codex, custom) do not bypass filesystem and write directly to project roots without going through detected files.
The boundary snapshot excludes output artifact roots correctly, so legitimate evidence writes do not trigger false positives.
risks: If boundary snapshot misses files (e.g., very large files, ignored paths, symlinks), mutations could occur undetected.
If agent provider has direct filesystem access that bypasses file walking (e.g., mounted volumes, special filesystems), mutations could occur outside snapshot detection.
If allowed_write_roots configuration is mis-specified, legitimate writes could block PRD readiness with false-positive mutations.

result.notes: Explorer confirms boundary audit implementation exists via snapshot comparison with enforcement blocking PRD readiness. Cross-check should verify snapshot completeness and agent provider isolation assumptions.
